title: Artificial Intelligence and Public Standards: government response to report
description: The government's response to the Committee's 2020 report Artificial Intelligence and Public Standards
summary: The government has responded to a committee's report on artificial intelligence and public standards by outlining its plans and intentions for this area. The policy document is a formal government response to advice given in a 2020 report.

title: Report and impact assessment on Copyright and Artificial Intelligence
description: A report and impact assessment on the use of copyright works in the development of artificial intelligence (AI) systems.
summary: This document is an assessment of how copyrights interact with the use of artificial intelligence, and its purpose is to identify and evaluate the implications of AI on copyright laws. It reports on the impact of AI on copyright works and their potential uses in AI systems.

title: Generative artificial intelligence (AI) in education
description: The Department for Education’s (DfE) position on the use of generative AI in the education sector.
summary: This document outlines the Department for Education's stance on using generative artificial intelligence in educational settings. It provides information on how AI technology affects education and possibly establishes guidelines or recommendations for its use.

title: Transport artificial intelligence action plan
description: Sets out how DfT will work with the transport sector to exploit the opportunities from AI while managing potential risks.
summary: This document outlines the Department for Transport's action plan for the integration of Artificial Intelligence (AI) in the transport sector. It focuses on utilizing AI's benefits while minimizing potential risks associated with its use in transportation.

title: Copyright and artificial intelligence: progress report
description: A progress report on the use of copyright works in the development of AI systems, under Section 137 of the Data (Use and Access) Act.
summary: This progress report discusses the use of copyrighted works in artificial intelligence (AI) system development. It appears to be an update on how copyrights are being applied to AI, likely in accordance with regulations under Section 137 of the Data (Use and Access) Act.

title: The regulation of artificial intelligence as a medical device: government response to the RHC
description: Government response to the recommendations made by the Regulatory Horizons Council (RHC) on the regulation of AI as a medical device.
summary: This government policy document outlines the government's response to recommendations on regulating artificial intelligence used in medical devices. The response aims to address the safety, efficacy, and accessibility of AI-powered medical devices.

title: ​​Ofqual’s approach to regulating the use of artificial intelligence in the qualifications sector​
description: Published in response to a request from the Secretary of State for Education and the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology.
summary: This is a policy document outlining Ofqual's guidelines for regulating the use of artificial intelligence in educational qualifications. It was created in response to government requests, providing a framework for responsible AI use.

title: Government response to the House of Lords Select Committee on Artificial Intelligence
description: Government response to the Lords Select Committee on Artificial Intelligence report ‘AI in the UK: No Room for Complacency’.
summary: This document outlines the UK government's response to recommendations from the House of Lords Select Committee on Artificial Intelligence, focusing on the development and regulation of artificial intelligence in the UK. It addresses concerns and suggestions from the committee's report 'AI in the UK: No Room for Complacency'.

title: AI and Public Standards – Terms of Reference
description: Terms of Reference and FAQs for the Committee's review into artificial intelligence and public standards.
summary: The terms of reference outline the framework for a committee to review the integration of artificial intelligence with public standards. The review aims to address any ethical and regulatory implications associated with AI.

title: CSPL submission to Science and Technology Committee inquiry on Governance of AI
description: CSPL has submitted written evidence to the Science and Technology Committee's inquiry on Governance of Artificial Intelligence.
summary: The Committee on Standards in Public Life (CSPL) has submitted evidence to an inquiry into how to govern Artificial Intelligence. This submission aims to contribute to a discussion on AI governance.
